texts.full_text: mor familial cancersyndrome, as well as parathyroid cancer and hyperparathyroidism. Cultured cells were introducedinto plastic capillarytubes andfrozen at 2000 atmospheres in a high-pressure freezingmachine to preserve their cellular ultrastructure.The frozen blocks were freeze-substituted in acetone with glutaraldehyde fixative,embedded in resin, sectioned, and labeled withprimaryantibodies against parafibromin.Specimens were reactedwith a secondary antibody conjugated to gold and wereimaged digitally in an energy-filtered transmissionelectron microscope (TEM). Electron micrographs showed that parafibromin wasconcentrated mainly in thenucleuswithsomelabeling elsewhere in the cell.

Here is an example of one of the lines: When I copied and pasted the line above that is what showed up, but the line on the looks like

oindent and \ are printed directly. "Good Instructor." comes from a variable. I tried converting the tex file to a txt file and now that line looks like this: 
oindent G o o d i n s t r u c t o r . \ Any ideas? A: Use system instead of !.

texts.full_text: it might be similar, but after a few months I realised it required a completely different set of skills. What didyou learn? This has been the ideal course for me, as, over the past few years, I’ve had the opportunity to develop a new team from scratch. Everything I needed to do, from thinking about what roles I required, conducting recruitment and selection, to setting objectives for PRDPs and managing successful teams, has been contained within the course. How has it been helpful in your role? I hope this programme has allowed me to give my team the freedo...

texts.full_text: signaling molecules, and cell cycle regulators. In addition to cochaperone association, ATP binding and hydrolysis, posttranslational modifications including phosphorylation, S‐nitrosylation, ethylation, and acetylation also regulate Hsp90 Generally, hyperacetylation decreases the binding of Hsp90 to ATP, cochaperone p23, and client proteins, resulting in decreased Hsp90 function.[5](#cas13748-bib-0005){ref-type="ref"}, [6](#cas13748-bib-0006){ref-type="ref"}, [7](#cas13748-bib-0007){ref-type="ref"} Because Hsp90 clients are fre‐quently involved in oncogenesis, the inhibition of Hsp90 is a promising strategy for cancer therapy.[8](#cas13748-bib-0008){ref-type="ref"} In many inhibitors have been developed that target the N‐terminal ATP binding site of Hsp90. These include natural products and radicicol), purine analogs, resorcinol derivatives, coumarin isoxazole compounds, and novobiocin.[9](#cas13748-bib-0009){ref-type="ref"} However, the clinical application of these inhibitors has been limited by toxicity. In addition to N‐terminal ATP binding site inhibition, C‐terminal Hsp90 inhibition has also been reported in preclinical studies;[10](#cas13748-bib-0010){ref-type="ref"}, [11](#cas13748-bib-0011){ref-type="ref"} and this strategy may be useful for the development of novel anticancer agents. Simvastatin, a 3‐hydroxy‐3‐methylglutaryl coenzyme A (HMG CoA) reductase inhibitor, has been widely used to treat hypercholesterolemia.[12](#cas13748-bib-0012){ref-type="ref"} In addition to its cholesterol lowering effect, simvastatin also exhibits anticancer activity against various cancer cell lines and animal models of xenografts,[13](#cas13748-bib-0013){ref-type="ref"}, [14](#cas13748-bib-0014){ref-type="ref"} including breast cancers.[15](#cas13748-bib-0015){ref-type="ref"} Triple‐negative breast cancer (TNBC) is a subtype of breast

texts.full_text: . 84-3039. United States Court of Appeals,Third Circuit. Argued Dec. 5, 1984.Decided March 29, 1985.Rehearing and Rehearing In BancDenied April 23, 1985. James L. Hymes, III (Argued), Charlotte Amalie, St. Thomas, V.I., for appellant. R. Eric Moore (Argued), Diane Trace Warlick, Christiansted, St. Croix, V.I., for appellee. Before SEITZ, GIBBONS and SLOVITER, Circuit Judges. OPINION OF THE COURT SLOVITER, Circuit Judge. 1 EaThe case of Eastern Airlines, Inc. v. Insurance Company of North America involves a dispute between the parties over an insurance coverage issue following aircraft damage sustained by Eastern Airlines. Background: Eastern Airlines (the appellee) had insured its fleet under policies issued by The Insurance Company of North America (INA), now known as CNA Financial Corporation, which is the appellant in this case. An incident occurred involving one of their planes at a location overseas where significant damage was sustained to an aircraft. The Legal Issue: Eastern Airlines sought indemnification from INA for repair costs and losses resulting from what they argued were covered events under their insurance policies with INA. The crux of the dispute lay in whether or not certain exclusions within those policies applied, t
